findings.reachability: Admin-only: only rendered for users with manage_options capability on the Plugins screen
findings.justification: The $url variable produced by admin_url() is interpolated into an <a href> attribute via sprintf() without esc_url(), violating WordPress secure-output conventions; in practice admin_url() is trusted and not user-controlled, but the missing escaping creates a latent risk if the function is ever filtered by another plugin to include attacker-controlled data.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: admin_url output without esc_url in sprintf
detection_pattern.description: Catches admin URL values passed to sprintf href slots without esc_url(). While admin_url() is normally safe, omitting esc_url() means any filter hooking admin_url can inject attribute-breaking content; also flags a consistent coding-standards gap that reviewers should note.
